briefdescription.para: Max Device Temperature Offset (Only for SE1 Silicon)
Min Device Temperature Offset (Only for SE1 Silicon)
Enable Average for Supply Reg Supply[0-31] Offset.
Enable Average for Supply Reg Supply[32-63] Offset.
Enable Average for Supply Reg Supply[64-95] Offset.
Enable Average for Supply Reg Supply[96-127] Offset.
Enable Average for Supply Reg Supply[127-159] Offset.
Enable Average for Temp Satellite Reg Satellite[1-32] Offset.
Enable Average for Temp Satellite Reg Satellite[33-64] Offset.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful else XST_FAILURE or an error code or a reason code.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the System Monitor device.

parameterdescription.para: Points to the XSysMonPsv device configuration structure.

simplesect.para: The user needs to first call the XSysMonPsv_LookupConfig() API which returns the Configuration structure pointer which is passed as a parameter to the XSysMonPsv_CfgInitialize() API.
None.

parameterdescription.para: Flag to enable or disable gate registers.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Sets the PMBus address for the System Monitor.

parameterdescription.para: Value at which the Sysmon will be addressed on the PMBus.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Enables/disbles the PMBus on the System Monitor.

parameterdescription.para: Value which enables or disables the PMBus.

simplesect.para: None.
If Enable = 0, PMBus lines will be in tristate. If Enable = 1, PMBus lines will be active.

briefdescription.para: Restricts or de-restrict all PMBs commands.

parameterdescription.para: Value which restrics or de-restrict the PMBus commands.

simplesect.para: None.
If Enable = 0, PMBus commands are disabled. If Enable = 1, PMBus commands are enabled.

briefdescription.para: Selects I2C or PMBus interface to which traffic from SMBus is routed.

parameterdescription.para: Value which determies where the SMBus traffic gets routed to.

simplesect.para: None.
If Interface = 1, I2C command interface is enabled. If Interface = 0, PMBus command interface is enabled.

briefdescription.para: Resets the Min and Max values of supplies and temperature to negative and positive saturation respectively.

parameterdescription.para: Resets the MIN and MAX values reached by the internal supplies since the last reset.
Resets the MIN and MAX values reached by the device since the last reset.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Returns the device temperature threshold for min and max values in signed Q8.7 format.

parameterdescription.para: Enum which indicates the type of threshold.

simplesect.para: Device Temperature threshold in signed Q8.7 format.
To get the value in Deg Celsius, use XSysMonPsv_FixedToFloat.

parameterdescription.para: Enum which indicates the type of threshold.

simplesect.para: None.
Use XSysMonPsv_FloatToFixed to get the raw ADC value.

briefdescription.para: Returns the OT temperature threshold for min and max values in signed Q8.7 format.

parameterdescription.para: Enum which indicates the type of threshold

simplesect.para: OT Temperature threshold in signed Q8.7 format.

parameterdescription.para: Enum which indicates the type of threshold.

simplesect.para: None.
Use XSysMonPsv_FloatToFixed to get the raw ADC value.

briefdescription.para: Returns the temperature values for the device in signed Q8.7 format.

parameterdescription.para: Enum which indicates the type of temperature value to be read.

simplesect.para: Temperature value requested. XSYSMONPSV_INVALID if invalid value requested.

XSYSMONPSV_VAL_VREF_MIN and XSYSMONPSV_VAL_VREF_MAX are only supported for ES1 silicon to get min and max temperature values. XSYSMONPSV_VAL_VREF_MIN and XSYSMONPSV_VAL_VREF_MAX are not supported on production silicon. Use XSYSMONPSV_VAL to get the current temperature value on production silicon. XSYSMONPSV_VAL is not supported on ES1 silicon.

briefdescription.para: Reads the raw value for Supply Threshold.

parameterdescription.para: Enum which indicates the desired supply to be read.
Enum which indicates the type of threshold.

simplesect.para: The raw Upper or Lower threshold of the supply XSYSMONPSV_INVALID if the supply hasn't been configured.

briefdescription.para: Reads the raw supply value for requested.

parameterdescription.para: Enum which indicates the desired supply to be read.
Type of reading for the supply.

simplesect.para: The raw values for min, max or the prevailing supply value. Invalid if the supply is not configured.

briefdescription.para: Checks if new data is available for a supply.

parameterdescription.para: Enum which indicates the desired supply.

simplesect.para: True if new data available. False if new data is not available. Invalid if the supply is not configured.
None.

briefdescription.para: Checks if the supply value has exceeded the set threshold values.

parameterdescription.para: Enum which indicates the desired supply.

simplesect.para: True if new data available. False if new data is not available. Invalid if the supply is not configured.

briefdescription.para: Sets the raw value for upper supply threshold.

parameterdescription.para: Enum which indicates the desired supply to be configured

simplesect.para: XSYSMONPSV_INVALID if the Supply hasn't been configured XST_SUCCESS otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Sets the raw value for Lower Supply Threshold.

parameterdescription.para: Enum which indicates the desired supply to be configured.

simplesect.para: XSYSMONPSV_INVALID if the Supply hasn't been configured XST_SUCCESS otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Sets the alarm mode for temperature alarms.

parameterdescription.para: Sets the Hysteresis or window mode. Mode = 1 Hysteresis Mode Mode = 0 Window Mode

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Sets the alarm mode for OT alarm.

parameterdescription.para: Sets the Hysteresis or Window mode. Mode = XSYSMONPSV_HYSTERESIS Hysteresis Mode Mode = XSYSMONPSV_WINDOW Window Mode

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Reads the current supply Alarm Configuration.

parameterdescription.para: Supply for which configuration is to be read.

simplesect.para: TRUE if enabled. FALSE if disabled. XSYSMONPSV_INVALID if invalid SupplyValue.

briefdescription.para: Sets the Alarm configuration for a supply.

parameterdescription.para: Supply for which config is to be set.
Alarm config value. XSYSMONPSV_ENABLE to enable XSYSMONPSV_DISABLE to disable

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful. XSYSMONPSV_INVALID if invalid SupplyValue.

briefdescription.para: Reads the temperature and returns value in degree celsius.
Reads the temperature and returns value in raw format.
Reads the temperature of each satellite in degree celsius.
Reads the temperature of each satellite in raw format.

parameterdescription.para: Upper threshold value to be read.
lower threshold value to be read.

briefdescription.para: Reads the voltage for the supply.
Reads the voltage for the supply in raw.
Sets the upper threshold voltage for the supply.
Sets loser threshold voltage for the supply.
Gets the upper threshold voltage for the supply.
Gets the lower threshold voltage for the supply.
Enables or Disables temperature satellites averaging.

parameterdescription.para: Temperature Satellite id, indexed from 1 to 64.

simplesect.para: None

briefdescription.para: Sets temperature satellites average sampling rate value.

parameterdescription.para: Average Sampling Rate Value to be set.

simplesect.para: None

briefdescription.para: Gets temperature satellites average sampling rate value.

parameterdescription.para: Average Sampling Rate Value to be read.

briefdescription.para: Enables or Disables voltage supplies averaging.
Sets voltage supplies average sampling rate value.

parameterdescription.para: Average Sampling Rate Value to be set.

simplesect.para: None

briefdescription.para: Gets voltage supplies average sampling rate value.

parameterdescription.para: Average Sampling Rate Value to be read.

briefdescription.para: Enables the specified interrupts in the device.

parameterdescription.para: 32 bit-mask of the interrupts to be enabled. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XSYSMONPSV_IER_* bits defined in xsysmonpsv_hw.h.
Interrupt enable register to be used.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Returns the enabled interrupts read from the Interrupt Mask Register (IMR).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XSYSMONPSV_IMR0_* and XSYSMONPSV_IMR1_* constants defined in xsysmonpsv_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: Interrupt enable register to be used

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Interrupt Mask Registers.

briefdescription.para: Disables the specified interrupts in the device.

parameterdescription.para: 32 bit-mask of the interrupts to be enabled. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XSYSMONPSV_IDR_* bits defined in xsysmonpsv_hw.h.
Interrupt disable register to be used.

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Returns the interrupt status read from Interrupt Status Register(ISR).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XSYSMONPSV_ISR* constants defined in xsysmonpsv_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

simplesect.para: A 32-bit value representing the contents of the Interrupt Status Register (ISR).

briefdescription.para: Clears the specified interrupts in the Interrupt Status Register (ISR).

parameterdescription.para: 32 bit-mask of the interrupts to be cleared. Bit positions of 1 will be cleared. Bit positions of 0 will not change the previous interrupt status.*

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Sets a supply as a source new data interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: 32 bit Mask for NEW_DATA_n fields in the interrupt registers

simplesect.para: None.

briefdescription.para: Looks for the device configuration based on the unique device ID.

detaileddescription.para: The table XSysmonPsu_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration information for each device in the system.

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device, or NULL if no match is found.

briefdescription.para: This macro reads the given register.

parameterdescription.para: Register address in the address space of the SYSMONPSV device.

simplesect.para: The 32-bit value of the register

briefdescription.para: This macro writes the given register.

parameterdescription.para: Register address in the address space of the SYSMONPSV device.
32-bit value to write to the register.

simplesect.para: None.

detaileddescription.para: by using protection macros

briefdescription.para: Functions in the xsysmonpsv_common.c file are basic driver functions which will be used in the in services or directly by the user.

detaileddescription.para: Functions in the xsysmonpsv_sinit.c file are the minimum required functions for the XSysMonPsv driver.
Functions in the xsysmonpsv_intr.c file are the minimum required functions for the XSysMonPsv driver.
Functions in the xsysmonpsv.c file are the minimum required functions for the XSysMonPsv driver.
The xsysmonpsv_services.h file contains Services layers APIs such as temperature and voltage alarms.
The xsysmonpsv_services.c file contains functions which provides services like temperature and voltage event notification on subscription basis.
The xsysmonpsv_secure.h header file contains low level driver functions that are use to read and write sysmon registers.
The xsysmonpsv_secure.c file contains basic driver functions.
The xsysmonpsv_lowlevel.h header file contains low level driver functions that are use to read and write sysmon registers.
The xsysmonpsv_hw.h header file contains the identifiers and basic driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the device.
SysMon driver supports the AMD System Monitor device on Versal.
The xsysmonpsv_direct.c file contains the function implementation for low level sysmon APIs.
Functions in the xsysmonpsv_common.h file are basic driver functions which will be used in the in services or directly by the user.

para.preformatted:
MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
3.0 cog 03/25/21 Driver Restructure 4.0 se 10/04/22 Update return value definitions se 10/27/22 Secure and Non-Secure mode integration 5.1 se 03/03/25 Compiler warnings fixed 5.3 dc 02/18/26 Correct spelling errors se 03/12/26 Enhancements: NULL checks, range validation, standardize return values to XST_FAILURE se 03/25/26 Fix Coverity/MISRA-C violations: U suffix, (void) cast, sign conversion, parentheses

MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
3.0 cog 03/25/21 Driver Restructure 4.0 se 10/04/22 Update return value definitions 5.2 se 08/24/25 Microblaze support added 5.3 dc 02/18/26 Correct spelling errors se 03/25/26 Fix Coverity/MISRA-C violations: U suffix, (void) cast, sign conversion, parentheses

MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
3.0 cog 03/25/21 Driver Restructure 4.0 se 11/10/22 Secure and Non-Secure mode integration 5.3 se 03/12/26 Enhancements: NULL checks, range validation, standardize return values to XST_FAILURE se 03/13/26 Fix secure mode and PCSR re-lock in SDT flow

detaileddescription.para:
The System Monitor device has the following features:
Measure and monitor up to 160 voltages across the chip
Automatic alarms based on user defined limis for the on-chip temperature.
Optional interrupt request generation
The user should refer to the hardware device specification for detailed information about the device.

para.preformatted:
MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
3.0 cog 03/25/21 Driver Restructure 3.1 cog 04/09/22 Remove GIC standalone related functionality for arch64 architecture 4.0 se 10/04/22 Update return value definitions 4.0 se 11/10/22 Secure and Non-Secure mode integration 4.1 cog 07/18/23 Add support for SDT flow 5.1 se 03/03/25 Compiler warnings fixed 5.2 se 08/24/25 Microblaze support added 5.3 se 03/13/26 Fix secure mode and PCSR re-lock in SDT flow

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 aad 11/09/18 First release 1.1 aad 07/11/19 Fixed NEW_DATA_MASK 2.0 aad 10/12/20 MISRAC Violations 2.1 aad 02/24/21 Added additional macro to support production silicon. 2.3 aad 07/26/21 Added doxygen comments for Macros aad 07/26/21 Removed unused Macros 3.0 cog 03/25/21 Driver Restructure 5.0 se 08/01/24 Added new APIs to enable, set and get averaging for voltage supplies and temperature satellites. 5.3 dc 02/18/26 Correct spelling errors se 03/25/26 Fix Coverity/MISRA-C violations: U suffix, (void) cast, sign conversion, parentheses

MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
3.0 cog 03/25/21 Driver Restructure 4.0 se 11/10/22 Secure and Non-Secure mode integration

MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
4.0 se 11/10/22 Secure and Non-Secure mode integration 5.3 se 03/12/26 Enhancements: NULL checks, range validation, standardize return values to XST_FAILURE se 03/13/26 Fix secure mode and PCSR re-lock in SDT flow

MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
4.0 se 11/10/22 Secure and Non-Secure mode integration 5.2 ak 03/10/25 Fixed doxygen warnings 5.3 se 03/25/26 Fix Coverity/MISRA-C violations: U suffix, (void) cast, sign conversion, parentheses

MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
3.0 cog 03/25/21 Driver Restructure 3.1 cog 04/09/22 Remove GIC standalone related functionality for arch64 architecture 4.0 se 10/04/22 Update return value definitions 4.0 se 11/10/22 Secure and Non-Secure mode integration 5.2 se 08/24/25 Microblaze support added 5.3 dc 02/18/26 Correct spelling errors se 03/12/26 Enhancements: NULL checks, range validation, standardize return values to XST_FAILURE se 03/25/26 Fix Coverity/MISRA-C violations: U suffix, (void) cast, sign conversion, parentheses

MODIFICATION HISTORY: Ver Who Date Changes
3.0 cog 03/25/21 Driver Restructure 3.1 cog 04/09/22 Remove GIC standalone related functionality for arch64 architecture 4.0 se 11/10/22 Secure and Non-Secure mode integration 5.2 se 08/24/25 Microblaze support added

detaileddescription.para: See xsysmonpsv.h for a detailed description of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 aad 11/20/18 First release. 1.2 aad 06/11/20 Corrected the configuration assignment 2.0 aad 07/31/20 Added new APIs to set threshold values, alarm config and modes for temperature and voltages 2.1 aad 02/24/21 Added documentation and support for production silicon. 2.3 aad 07/26/21 Fixed doxygen comments. 3.0 cog 03/25/21 Driver Restructure 3.1 cog 04/09/22 Remove GIC standalone related functionality for arch64 architecture 4.0 se 10/04/22 Update return value definitions se 11/10/22 Secure and Non-Secure mode integration 5.0 se 08/01/24 Added new APIs to enable, set and get averaging for voltage supplies and temperature satellites. 5.2 se 08/24/25 Microblaze support added 5.3 dc 02/18/26 Correct spelling errors se 03/12/26 Enhancements: NULL checks, range validation, standardize return values to XST_FAILURE se 03/25/26 Fix Coverity/MISRA-C violations: U suffix, (void) cast, sign conversion, parentheses

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 aad 02/08/18 First release 1.2 aad 06/14/20 Fixed temperature calculation for negative temp 2.0 aad 07/31/20 Added new APIs to set threshold values, alarm config and modes for temperature and voltages. Added new interrupt handling structure. aad 10/12/20 Fixed MISRAC violations 2.1 aad 02/24/21 Added additional documentation to support production silicon. 2.2 aad 03/29/21 Added an array that contains the supply names in strings. 2.3 aad 04/30/21 Size optimization for PLM code. 2.3 aad 07/26/21 Added doxygen comments. 2.3 aad 09/01/21 Fixed compilation warning. 3.0 cog 03/25/21 Driver Restructure 3.1 cog 04/09/22 Remove GIC standalone related functionality for arch64 architecture 4.0 se 10/04/22 Update return value definitions 5.0 se 08/01/24 Added new APIs to enable, set and get averaging for voltage supplies and temperature satellites. 5.2 se 08/24/25 Microblaze support added

detaileddescription.para: See xsysmonpsv.h for a detailed description of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 aad 11/20/18 First release. 1.3 aad 06/23/20 Fixed the register to read enabled interrupts. 2.0 aad 02/10/20 Added new Interrupt handler structure. 2.3 aad 04/30/21 Size optimization for PLM. aad 07/26/21 Fixed doxygen comments. aad 09/21/21 Fixed warning. 3.0 cog 03/25/21 Driver Restructure 3.1 cog 04/09/22 Remove GIC standalone related functionality for arch64 architecture 4.0 se 11/10/22 Secure and Non-Secure mode integration 5.2 se 08/24/25 Microblaze support added 5.3 dc 02/18/26 Correct spelling errors

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 aad 20/11/18 First release. 2.3 aad 07/26/21 Fixed doxygen comments. 3.0 cog 03/25/21 Driver Restructure 4.1 cog 07/18/23 Add support for SDT flow 4.2 cog 02/27/24 Fixed issue with XSysMonPsv_LookupConfig
